top_categories.workout_context: 75 miles on mixed terrain burns energy faster than the distance suggests. Train the fade resistance now.
79ft/mi means long efforts at or near FTP on every climb. If your threshold cracks at minute 15, you'll walk the rest.
At Fuji Panorama Gran Fondo, surges push you above threshold and you have to recover while still riding. Over-unders train exactly that.
5,906ft of climbing means repeated surges above threshold. VO2max work is how you survive the fifth climb, not just the first.
Race-pace efforts that mimic Fuji Panorama Gran Fondo's demands. Pacing, fueling, and tactical decisions under fatigue. Practice the race before race day.
Base fitness for Fuji Panorama Gran Fondo. Without this, the hard workouts break you down instead of building you up.
88–92% FTP is where you'll spend the hardest sustained miles of Fuji Panorama Gran Fondo. Train it until it feels boring.
Fuji Panorama Gran Fondo's mixed terrain forces constant power changes. Surge over the rough stuff, settle on the smooth, repeat for 75 miles.
5,906ft of climbing on grades that change every minute. You need to switch from seated to standing without losing rhythm.
The aerobic base that lets you ride mixed terrain at tempo for 75 miles without cracking. No shortcuts.
Double-threshold training builds sustained power with less fatigue. More FTP per hour of training.
Endurance with intensity spikes baked in. Exactly what 5,906ft of climbing on mixed terrain does to your power file on race day.

race_overlay.heat: Complete 3–4 heat exposure sessions in the final 2 weeks before Fuji Panorama Gran Fondo. Increase sodium intake 48 hours before race day.
race_overlay.nutrition: Target 40–60g carbs/hour for Fuji Panorama Gran Fondo’s 75 miles. Front-load calories in the first half. One bottle per hour minimum, more in heat.
race_overlay.altitude: Moderate altitude at Fuji Panorama Gran Fondo. Arrive 2–3 days early. Reduce intensity expectations by 5–10%. Hydrate aggressively.

pack_summary: This 10-workout pack focuses on Durability, TT Threshold, and Over Under to prepare you for 75 miles of mixed terrain in Lake Saiko, Yamanashi Prefecture, Japan.
